config.title: Berlin Plug Adapter - FAQ
config.subtitle: Quick answers about Berlin sockets, Germany voltage, travel adapters, USB-C chargers and power banks.

items.q: What plug adapter do I need for Berlin?
items.a: Most tourists from outside continental Europe need a **Type F / Europe plug adapter** for Berlin. Germany uses Type F / Schuko sockets. If your charger already has a slim two-pin Europlug or German-style Schuko plug, it will usually fit.

items.q: Do I need a voltage converter in Berlin?
items.a: You usually do **not** need a voltage converter for modern phone, laptop, tablet and camera chargers if the label says **Input: 100-240V, 50/60Hz**. You do need caution if a device says only 110V or 120V, because Berlin wall power is 230V.

items.q: Will my iPhone or laptop charger work in Berlin?
items.a: Most modern phone and laptop chargers work with Berlin voltage, but the wall plug may not fit. Check the charger brick for 100-240V. If it has that range, use a Type F adapter or buy a Berlin-compatible USB-C wall charger.

items.q: Can I use my hair dryer or straightener in Berlin?
items.a: Only if the label clearly supports 220-240V or 100-240V. Hair dryers and straighteners draw a lot of power, so a small plug adapter alone is not enough for a 110/120V-only model. The safer plan is a dual-voltage tool, the hotel dryer, or buying locally.

items.q: Can I bring a power bank to Berlin?
items.a: Yes, but keep it in hand luggage when flying. Lithium power banks are normally not allowed in checked luggage. Check your airline limits for very large batteries.

items.q: Where can I buy a plug adapter in Berlin?
items.a: Try BER airport shops, Berlin Hauptbahnhof, Alexanderplatz, electronics stores, larger drugstores and hotel reception. A Späti may help with a cable, but it is not a reliable plan for the exact adapter you need.
